mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases when industrial production grows, or the predictive power vanishes during contractionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 235.0 while the federal funds rate stays low, markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of periods where inflation exceeds 235.0 and rates are below 1.0%, or the interaction effect reverses sign.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates economic stagnation and compressed corporate profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal fails to predict negative returns, with positive 20-day returns occurring in >65% of sub-1.5% GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment confirms a robust supply-side recovery without labor market overheating, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the joint signal loses predictive accuracy during supply-constrained inflationary periods.
